front: Author: in The Mutation of Fortune, this author turns from a woman into a 'complete, natural man' after her husband's death, citing Iphis and Tiresias
Author: in The Path of Long Study, this author reads The Consolation of Philosophy before being led on a dream-journey by the Cumaean Sibyl
Author: this author's letter to Jean de Montreuil attacking Jean de Meun's immorality kicked off the 'Quarrel of the Rose'
Author: this author wrote the Epistle of Othea to Hector, in which Penthesilea embodies charity
Author: this medieval author's final written work was a poem dedicated to Joan of Arc
Author: this author's best-known elegiac ballade begins 'Alone am I and alone I wish to be'
Author: this author defined a 'just war' as one fought to achieve peace without pillaging in The Book of Feats of Arms and Chivalry
Author: this author served as court writer for several Dukes of Burgundy, including John the Fearless
